data.title: This week's top mining news highlights: Mining company MARA transferred out another 250 BTC, bringing its total sales in March to 15,133 BTC.
data.description: Week 16 of 2026 (April 11-April 17): 1. According to cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 978.9 EH/s, with a high of 1132.9 EH/s and a low of 849.6 EH/s, a 2.57% increase from the previous week's average hashrate (1045 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $73,155.9, with a high of $76,127.18 and a low of $70,512.7, a 6% increase from the previous week's average price ($68,863). 3. Mining news worth noting includes: (1) Bitcoin mining company Riot Platforms is suspected of selling another 500 BTC; (2) Mining company MARA transferred out another 250 BTC, bringing the total number of BTC sold in March to 15,133; (3) Bitcoin miners are facing the 2028 halving: profits are under pressure, energy is becoming tight, and the industry is turning to "infrastructure"; Data partner: NYSE-listed Bitcoin mining company Cango Inc. (CANG).

data.title: Mint Blockchain Announces Shutdown and Urges Asset Transfer by October 2026
data.description:
Mint Blockchain has announced the cessation of its operations as of April 17, 2026. According to BlockBeats, the blockchain network has advised users to transfer their assets to the Ethereum mainnet to ensure their security. The assets involved include ETH, WBTC, USDC, and USDT.

The announcement specifies that users must complete the asset migration through the official extraction channel by October 20, 2026. Until this deadline, users can continue to withdraw their assets to ensure a safe transfer to external networks. Failure to complete the withdrawal by the specified date will result in the assets being irretrievable.

data.title: Data: ETH total 24-hour contract trading volume increased by 11.10%.
data.description: According to CoinGlass data, the total trading volume of ETH futures contracts increased by 11.10% in the past 24 hours, currently standing at $51.941 billion. Binance accounted for $10.691 billion, OKX for $9.098 billion, and Gate for $5.71 billion. Meanwhile, the total open interest of ETH futures contracts is approximately $32.242 billion. Binance holds $4.889 billion and Gate holds $3.804 billion, ranking first and second globally.

data.title: ZetaChain integrates with the Claude Opus 4.7 model.
data.description: ZetaChain announced that it has integrated with Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 model and deployed it in its ecosystem applications. The model features proxy-based encoding capabilities, long-term task stability, and higher-resolution visual processing capabilities. ZetaChain stated that it is building an access layer for AI models while emphasizing the privacy and portability of user data.

data.title: Superstate Launches FundOS to Enhance On-Chain Fund Deployment
data.description: Superstate, a fintech company specializing in cryptocurrency, has introduced a new fund operating system called FundOS. According to Foresight News, FundOS opens the underlying infrastructure used by Superstate's proprietary funds to asset management companies, facilitating on-chain deployment for traditional private equity funds, public funds, and ETFs. Fund managers can maintain their existing operational methods while selecting supported blockchains, DeFi protocols, and stablecoin subscription/redemption options. Currently, FundOS provides technical support for USTB and USCC, managing assets exceeding $1 billion, and has been adopted by Invesco US.

data.title: OCBC Leads Bid for HSBC's Indonesian Retail Assets
data.description: OCBC has reportedly become the leading contender to acquire HSBC's retail assets in Indonesia, according to sources familiar with the matter. Bloomberg posted on X that the Singapore-based bank aims to strengthen its foothold in Southeast Asia's largest economy through this acquisition. The move aligns with OCBC's strategic expansion plans in the region, as it seeks to capitalize on Indonesia's growing financial market. HSBC's decision to sell its retail operations in Indonesia is part of its broader strategy to streamline its global operations and focus on more profitable markets. The acquisition, if successful, would mark a significant step for OCBC in enhancing its regional presence and competitiveness.

data.title: World ID 4.0 Introduces Fee System for Proof of Human Certificates
data.description: World has announced the introduction of a fee system for its World ID 4.0, according to Foresight News. The new system allows certificate issuers, such as the World Foundation, and protocol layers to charge applications for using Proof of Human certificates, while ensuring that end users remain unaffected by these fees. The fee structure includes issuer fees, set by the certificate issuers, and protocol base fees. Applications can pay through pre-funded wallets, with Web3 native applications able to make payments directly on-chain, and Web2 platforms settling via third-party fiat transactions. This mechanism aims to create sustainable revenue for the World protocol and certificate issuers, fostering a positive growth cycle.

data.title: Former U.S. Treasury Secretary Warns of Potential U.S. Treasury Demand Collapse
data.description:
Former U.S. Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson has issued a warning about the potential collapse in demand for U.S. Treasuries, urging authorities to prepare short-term, targeted emergency plans. According to ChainCatcher, Paulson emphasized that such a crisis could have severe impacts. The U.S. Treasury market is considered a cornerstone of the global financial system, and instability could affect the pricing of corporate debt, mortgages, and stocks.

The report highlights that a crisis weakening confidence in the U.S. dollar might drive funds towards alternative value storage assets like Bitcoin and gold. However, Tether, which uses U.S. Treasuries as a primary reserve asset, could face redemption and de-pegging risks.

data.title: Huma Finance Delays HUMA Token Unlocks by Six Months
data.description: Huma Finance has announced a six-month delay in the unlock schedule for its HUMA tokens allocated to team members and investors. According to NS3.AI, the initial unlock date, originally set for May 26, has been postponed to November 26. In addition to this delay, the project is planning to introduce a deflationary mechanism and new utility features within the year. Furthermore, Huma Finance aims to implement on-chain governance in the fourth quarter.

data.title: Polymarket Betting on 2026 Elections Surpasses $2 Billion
data.description: Polymarket's platform has seen betting on the 2026 elections exceed $2 billion. According to ChainCatcher, the upcoming elections in Hungary and Peru have contributed to a significant increase in betting volume in April. The share of bets from non-U.S. markets has risen substantially, now accounting for the majority. The Block highlights that election prediction markets are evolving from a U.S.-centric topic to a global phenomenon.

data.title: OpenAI Codex Celebrates Anniversary with Usage Limit Reset
data.description: OpenAI has announced a reset of usage limits for all Codex packages in celebration of its one-year anniversary. According to Odaily, Thibault Sottiaux, the engineering lead for OpenAI Codex, shared the news on the X platform. The announcement was made using Codex's newly introduced Computer Use feature, demonstrating the operation by clicking the 'RESET' button in the browser.

data.title: Arthur Hayes' linked address deposited 3,000 ETH into Binance and Bybit, and still holds 5,278 ETH.
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ai (@ai_9684xtpa), an address associated with Arthur Hayes deposited 3,000 ETH, worth approximately $6.927 million, to Binance and Bybit 11 hours ago. On-chain data shows that the Bybit deposit address used by this address had interacted with Arthur Hayes a year ago. This address currently holds 5,278 ETH, worth approximately $12.33 million.

data.title: The new address spent 7.45 million USDC to buy 168,800 HYPE tokens, some of which have already been staked.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a newly created address deposited $7.45 million USDC into Hyperliquid in the past 3 hours and purchased 168,837 HYPE tokens at an average price of approximately $44. Of these, approximately 60,000 HYPE tokens (approximately $2.61 million) have been used for staking.

data.title: OpenAI resets all Codex subscription limits to celebrate its first anniversary.
data.description: Thibault Sottiaux, Engineering Lead at OpenAI Codex, stated on the X platform that to celebrate Codex's first anniversary, the company has reset the usage limits for all plans. He explained that this announcement was made through Codex's newly launched Computer Use feature, where the model clicked the "RESET" button in the browser to complete the demonstration.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 630 USDT with a 0.85% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 17, 2026, 02:38 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 630 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 630.109985 USDT, with a narrowed 0.85% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: BRC-20 Tokens Experience Significant Surge in Market Value
data.description:
The BRC-20 token sector has seen a notable increase in market value, according to data from Coingecko. According to ChainCatcher, the market capitalization of this sector has surpassed $237 million, with a 24-hour growth rate of 135.2% and trading volume nearing $1.6 billion.

Among the tokens, MUBI is currently priced at $0.001429, marking a 24-hour increase of 398.9%. ORDI is valued at $9.15, reflecting a 144.7% rise. SATS is priced at $0.00000002266, with a 60.8% increase, while TURT stands at $0.0002204, up by 36.6%. Additionally, 1000 SATS is priced at $0.00002215, showing a 59.8% increase.

data.title: CITIC Lyon Raises CATL Target Price Amid Strong Q1 Earnings
data.description: CITIC Lyon has raised its target price for CATL (03750.HK) from HK$710 to HK$740, marking a 4% increase, while reaffirming its 'high conviction outperform' investment rating. According to Jin10, despite the rise in lithium prices since the beginning of the year, CATL's earnings for the first quarter of the 2026 fiscal year slightly exceeded expectations, which CITIC Lyon views positively. With the release of the first-quarter results, market attention may shift to CATL's Technology Day event next Tuesday, April 21, which management describes as the largest event in recent years. Additionally, CITIC Lyon has raised its earnings per share forecasts for CATL for the 2026-2028 period by 2-3%.

data.title: Ethereum NFT Platform Foundation Permanently Closes
data.description:
Ethereum NFT marketplace Foundation has announced its permanent closure. According to ChainCatcher, founder Kayvon Tehranian revealed on X that the platform was initially intended to be sold to a buyer interested in continuing its operations, but the deal fell through, leading the company to decide against seeking other buyers. The platform's infrastructure has been shut down with no plans to relaunch.

In January, Tehranian transferred ownership of Foundation to digital art display company BlackDove, citing the move as beneficial for the platform's long-term development. However, after completing the operational handover, BlackDove conducted a thorough due diligence and concluded that building its proprietary marketplace was more feasible, leading to the termination of their collaboration.

Since its launch, Foundation facilitated approximately $230 million in primary sales and hosted notable auctions featuring artists like Jen Stark, James Jean, and Edward Snowden. The closure has reignited concerns about the durability of NFTs hosted on centralized infrastructures.

data.title: OpenAI plans to invest over $20 billion over three years to purchase Cerebras servers and acquire equity.
data.description: OpenAI plans to invest over $20 billion over the next three years to purchase servers powered by Cerebras chips, and will acquire equity in the company as part of the deal. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Flow Capital to Launch $150 Million Private Credit Fund on Blockchain Platform
data.description: Flow Capital is set to introduce its $150 million private credit fund on a blockchain platform based in Singapore by the end of the month. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move positions Flow Capital as one of the first Asian managers to leverage the increasing liquidity in stablecoins. The initiative reflects a growing trend among financial managers to utilize blockchain technology for enhanced transparency and efficiency in fund management. This strategic decision aims to capitalize on the burgeoning interest in digital assets and stablecoins within the financial sector.
